text.title: django-service-layer Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure to extract business logic into a service function.

step.name: extract
step.input: view / Celery task / admin action containing write logic
step.action: Identify every mutation (`save`, `update`, `delete`, `bulk_create`) plus side effects. Cut them from the caller; paste into a function in `apps/<app>/services.py`.
step.output: draft service function with raw logic
step.decision-gate: Caller now contains only orchestration (serializer validation + service call + response).

step.name: contract
step.action: Convert signature to kwarg-only: `def <verb>_<entity>(*, …)`. Replace `**kwargs` with explicit parameters. Add type hints + docstring.
step.decision-gate: No `**kwargs`; every parameter has a type hint.

step.name: atomic
step.action: If multiple model writes are present, wrap them in `with transaction.atomic():`. Replace `Model.objects.create(...)` with `Model(...); full_clean(); save()` for instances that have `clean()` defined.
step.decision-gate: A test that raises an exception inside the atomic block leaves zero rows committed.

step.name: domain-exceptions
step.input: existing HTTP/DRF exception sites within the service
step.action: Replace each with the corresponding domain exception (`NotFoundError`, `ValidationError`, `PermissionDeniedError`, `ApplicationError`). Add a DRF exception handler in `core/exception_handler.py` mapping domain → HTTP if not present.

step.name: on-commit
step.input: side-effect calls (Celery, email, webhook) currently inside the atomic block
step.action: Wrap each as `transaction.on_commit(lambda: <effect>)`. Move the call outside the atomic block (still inside the service function) if the effect is read-only.
step.output: side effects fire only after commit
step.decision-gate: A test that rolls back the transaction does NOT trigger the side effect.
